frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: Current percept attracted toward a recently seen stimulus along the same feature dimension.

core_question: Does attractive serial dependence transfer across distinct stimulus dimensions (numerosity and duration) of the same visual event?
key_assumptions: Numerosity and duration are perceptually associated and can bias each other
Serial dependence can be probed by an inducer presented before a reference
manipulations: Inducer varied simultaneously in both duration and numerosity
key_findings: Attractive serial dependence appeared only within the task-relevant dimension
Numerosity history biased numerosity only; duration history biased duration only
In the numerosity task, the task-irrelevant duration of the inducer produced an opposite, repulsive effect
interpretations: Attractive serial dependence is dimension-specific and does not transfer across stimulus dimensions
Cross-dimensional history effects can be repulsive rather than attractive
